content: Building Your Personal Brand as a Founder: Your personal brand is the intersection of your expertise, your story, and your consistent presence. The brand-building stack for founders: Choose one platform and go deep before expanding. Twitter/X for ideas and reach. LinkedIn for B2B and investor visibility. YouTube for deep authority. Newsletter for owned audience. The content hierarchy: Level 1 — Share what you are learning (easiest, builds credibility). Level 2 — Share your opinions on your industry (harder, builds authority). Level 3 — Share your story including failures (hardest, builds trust). The compound content law: consistency over 12 months beats viral moments. Show up every week with something genuinely useful. Your audience becomes your best customers, referrers, and eventually investors.

Monetising Expertise in the AI Economy: The five monetisation models for knowledge creators: 1. Cohort courses ($500-$3000 per student, high-touch, 3-8 weeks). 2. Productised consulting (fixed-scope, fixed-price deliverables). 3. Community membership ($30-$200/month recurring). 4. Digital products (templates, frameworks, toolkits — $20-$500 each). 5. AI-powered services (use AI to deliver high-volume outputs at consulting-level quality). The sequencing advice: Start with 1:1 consulting to understand your market. Package the insights into a course. Build the community around the course alumni. Create digital products from your most-asked questions. Each stage informs the next. Your first revenue should come from a person, not a product.
